response.justification: Strong polyglot engineer with directly relevant high-throughput, distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of requests/second with async I/O, caching, and fibers) and current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows — an excellent match for the AI-native mindset this role treats as baseline. Prior Staff-level titles and deep software-craftsmanship practice (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based) support the seniority bar. However, the most explicit gap is the inverted language fit: the candidate is strong in TypeScript (only a working-level requirement) but lists no Python at all, which is a stated must-have. Operational readiness/on-call and cross-functional stakeholder management are also not evidenced. A screen is warranted to verify Python depth and operational experience before deciding.
strengths: Current AI-agent infra / MCP / agentic workflow experience matches the AI-native baseline
Proven high-throughput distributed systems (millions of req/s, low-latency async I/O)
Staff-level history plus strong craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, mentoring)
concerns: No Python anywhere despite it being a 'strong Python' must-have
No explicit on-call, incident-response, or operational-ownership evidence
Heavy investment in niche DLang; agile/stakeholder coordination only implied
